title: Bitter French Mentaiko Tartiflette
description: A hearty, chewy, meal-prep dish from Brittany, France, featuring the bold flavor of mentaiko. Perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Caramelize the onions by cooking them in a pan over low heat for 20 minutes.
Cook the lardons in a pan over medium heat until crispy.
Peel and dice the potatoes, then boil them in salted water until tender.
Mash the potatoes with a fork and set aside.
In a separate pan, combine the mentaiko, garlic, and thyme. Cook over low heat for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Stir the cooked mentaiko mixture into the mashed potatoes.
Add the caramelized onions and cooked lardons to the potato mixture. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and cook in the preheated oven for 20 minutes.
Serve hot, garnished with additional thyme if desired.
